sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Bayer 04 Leverkusen are in solid form, winning 3 of their last 5 matches, including a convincing 3-1 victory over RB Leipzig. VfB Stuttgart have also shown resilience, remaining unbeaten in their last 4 games, with a notable 4-0 win against SV Werder Bremen. Both teams are performing well, but Leverkusen’s attacking prowess at home gives them a slight edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Leverkusen have had the upper hand, securing a thrilling 4-3 win in March 2025 and a 0-0 draw in November 2024. Historically, matches between these sides are high-scoring, with 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads seeing over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in 6 of 7 recent clashes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Leverkusen rank 3rd with 29 points and are second in the league for goals scored (33), showcasing their attacking strength. Stuttgart, sitting 6th with 26 points, have a balanced attack (25 goals) but struggle with consistency away from home. Leverkusen’s superior ball possession (59.5%) and big chances created (57) suggest they will dominate play.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Leverkusen’s Alejandro Grimaldo (7.48 avg. rating, 4 goals, 4 assists) and Aleix García (7.39 avg. rating, 4 assists) are in fine form. For Stuttgart, Deniz Undav (8 goals, 2nd in league) and Jamie Leweling (5 assists) pose significant threats. Leverkusen’s depth could be decisive despite missing key players.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Leverkusen’s high corner count (5.27 per game) and Stuttgart’s tendency for late goals (40.9% in 76-90 min) could shape the game. With Leverkusen’s strong final third entries and Stuttgart’s defensive vulnerabilities on the road, expect the hosts to press aggressively.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Leverkusen’s home advantage, unbeaten streak of 15 games against Stuttgart, and Sofascore’s 48% home win probability, they are favorites to win. Stuttgart’s recent form suggests they can score, but Leverkusen’s attacking output should secure a narrow victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, VfB Stuttgart holds a commanding 4-0 lead over Bayer 04 Leverkusen at the BayArena. Stuttgart has been ruthless in front of goal, capitalizing on their chances with clinical finishing. Leverkusen, despite dominating possession, has failed to convert their opportunities into goals, leaving them with a mountain to climb in the second half. The away side's efficiency in attack has been the defining factor so far, while Leverkusen's defense has looked vulnerable and unable to cope with Stuttgart’s sharpness.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum breakdown
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in attack during the first half heavily favored Stuttgart in the early stages, with consistent attacking spells that led to their opening goal. Leverkusen struggled to find rhythm initially but showed signs of life with sporadic bursts of pressure around the midway point. However, their momentum was disrupted as Stuttgart regained control during key moments, leading to additional goals. Towards the end of the half, Leverkusen pushed harder with sustained attacking intent, but Stuttgart delivered a decisive blow with late strikes to seal their dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For VfB Stuttgart, Jamie Leweling has been the standout performer, finding the net twice and posing a constant threat. Deniz Undav has also been instrumental, contributing with a goal and assists, showcasing his ability to influence the game in multiple ways. On the Leverkusen side, players like Martin Terrier and Ezequiel Fernández have shown glimpses of potential in attack, but they’ve lacked the final touch to make an impact. Defensively, Leverkusen has been exposed, with low individual ratings reflecting their struggles at the back.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Stuttgart’s approach has been highly effective, focusing on quick transitions and exploiting Leverkusen’s defensive lapses. Their ability to create big chances and convert them has been remarkable, while their defensive structure has held firm despite less possession. Leverkusen, on the other hand, has controlled the ball for longer periods but lacked creativity in the final third. Their inability to turn possession into dangerous opportunities has been a glaring issue, and defensively, they’ve been caught out by Stuttgart’s pace and precision.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Leverkusen will likely push hard to salvage something from this match, potentially leaving spaces at the back for Stuttgart to exploit on the counter. The home side’s desperation for goals could lead to a more open game, but their confidence may be shaken after such a heavy deficit. Stuttgart, with their lead, might adopt a more conservative stance to protect their advantage, but their attacking talent suggests they could still strike if given the chance. The outcome will hinge on whether Leverkusen can find a spark in attack and tighten up defensively.
